title: GGX Documentation
description: Documentation for GenGuardX, a Responsible AI governance platform for testing, approving, monitoring, and governing GenAI systems.

pages.title: GenGuardX (GGX)
pages.description: GenGuardX is a Responsible AI governance platform that helps enterprises test, approve, monitor, and govern customer-facing GenAI from pilot to production.

pages.title: Deployment and Monitoring
pages.description: Deploy approved GGX pipeline artifacts to production and monitor reliability, performance, accuracy, alerts, and human annotation queues for live GenAI systems.

pages.title: Annotation Queues
pages.description: Use GGX Annotation Queues to bring human review into production monitoring, label live data, track ground truth, and turn reviewer feedback into auditable metrics.

pages.title: CICD & Direct to Production
pages.description: Export approved GGX artifacts into production runtimes, APIs, containers, serverless services, or CI/CD systems without requiring GGX in the production environment.

pages.title: Governance Oversight
pages.description: Monitor GGX governance activity with dashboards, object views, custom alerts, role-based views, and review signals across registered models, prompts, RAGs, and pipelines.

pages.title: Performance Tracking
pages.description: Track approved GGX object performance with recurring jobs, metrics dashboards, thresholds, alerts, and data views that surface production and evaluation trends.

pages.title: Evaluations and Approval
pages.description: Evaluate GGX pipelines and components with automated jobs, human annotations, standardized reports, comparison workflows, and structured approval processes before production release.

pages.title: Approval Workflows
pages.description: Create GGX approval workflows with responsibilities, reviewers, review actions, comments, notifications, and audit trails for governed GenAI release decisions.

pages.title: Comparisons
pages.description: Compare GGX objects against challengers using shared datasets, selected metrics, automated reports, and side-by-side results for model and pipeline evaluation.

pages.title: Document Generation
pages.description: Generate governance and approval documentation from GGX evaluation results, metadata, reports, object details, and review evidence for downstream stakeholders.

pages.title: Feedback Portals
pages.description: Create GGX Feedback Portals that let testers and domain experts interact with pipelines, define expected outputs, collect session feedback, and review structured quality signals.

pages.title: Human Integrated Testing
pages.description: Run human-integrated GGX tests where reviewers evaluate pipeline behavior, capture qualitative feedback, validate outcomes, and support approval decisions.

pages.title: Reporting
pages.description: How reports are built in GGX — metadata, data schema, parameter schema, computation, and visualization — plus how to register, test, and reuse them.

pages.title: Simulation
pages.description: Run GGX simulation jobs on datasets to evaluate objects, generate reports, inspect metrics, validate evaluators, and compare results before approval.

pages.title: Frequently Asked Questions
pages.description: Common questions about evaluating and monitoring GenAI applications in GGX — production monitoring, reports and dashboards, LLM-as-a-judge metrics, reusable assets, data and integrations, governance and testing, versioning, and human feedback.

pages.title: GGX Integrations
pages.description: Connect GGX with LLM providers, LLM gateways, observability tools, agent frameworks, report providers, voice providers, and SSO systems across the AI lifecycle.

pages.title: Cleanlab
pages.description: Connect Cleanlab with GGX to use external data quality and evaluation signals in model, RAG, pipeline, and dataset assessment workflows.

pages.title: LLM Gateways
pages.description: Use LLM gateways with GGX by registering gateway-backed models in the Model Registry and routing requests to any LLM the gateway can access.

pages.title: Cloudflare AI Gateway
pages.description: Register Cloudflare AI Gateway as a GGX Model Registry model and route GGX requests to providers available through your Cloudflare gateway.

pages.title: Databricks AI Gateway
pages.description: Compare Databricks Unity AI Gateway with GGX for GenAI governance, automated compliance testing, approval evidence, production monitoring, and risk management.

pages.title: LiteLLM Gateway
pages.description: Register LiteLLM as a GGX Model Registry model and route GGX requests to any LLM provider configured behind LiteLLM.

pages.title: OpenRouter Gateway
pages.description: Register OpenRouter as a GGX Model Registry model and route GGX requests to OpenRouter-supported models through a unified API.

pages.title: Portkey Gateway
pages.description: Register Portkey as a GGX Model Registry model and use Portkey provider routing with GGX evaluations, pipelines, approvals, and monitoring.

pages.title: Setting up Integrations
pages.description: Configure LLM provider integrations in GGX by adding API keys, testing connections, and exposing secure environment variables for model registration and model code.

pages.title: Anthropic Integration
pages.description: Configure Anthropic in GGX and register Claude-backed models with provider credentials, model arguments, scoring logic, and governed model metadata.

pages.title: AWS Bedrock Integration
pages.description: Configure AWS Bedrock in GGX and register Bedrock-backed foundation models with AWS credentials, provider settings, model arguments, and scoring logic.

pages.title: Azure AI Integration
pages.description: Configure Azure AI in GGX and register Azure-hosted OpenAI models with API credentials, model provider settings, arguments, and scoring logic.

pages.title: Google Cloud Vertex AI Integration
pages.description: Configure Google Cloud Vertex AI in GGX and register Gemini models with service credentials, provider settings, input arguments, and scoring logic.

pages.title: Hugging Face Integration
pages.description: Register Hugging Face models in GGX using Hub models, optional tokens, model metadata, input arguments, and custom Python scoring logic.

pages.title: OpenAI Integration
pages.description: Configure OpenAI in GGX and register OpenAI-backed models with API credentials, model settings, arguments, and governed scoring logic.

pages.title: Observability
pages.description: Connect AI observability traces from LangSmith, Arize Phoenix, Langfuse, Humanloop, Datadog, and other tools to GGX for judges, human review, ground truth, bug categorization, and closed-loop AI lifecycle governance.

pages.title: Arize Phoenix
pages.description: Connect Arize Phoenix traces, evaluations, datasets, and human annotations to GGX for governed monitoring, review, bug categorization, and AI lifecycle improvement.

pages.title: Datadog
pages.description: Connect Datadog LLM Observability traces and incidents to GGX for AI judges, human review, ground truth creation, bug categorization, and lifecycle governance.

pages.title: Humanloop
pages.description: Connect Humanloop logs, evaluations, prompt management, and human feedback workflows to GGX for closed-loop AI lifecycle governance.

pages.title: Langfuse
pages.description: Use Langfuse traces, scores, datasets, and annotation queues with GGX to close the monitoring loop across judges, human review, ground truth, bugs, and approvals.

pages.title: LangSmith
pages.description: Use LangSmith traces with GGX to run judges, human reviews, ground truth promotion, bug tracking, and closed-loop AI monitoring.

pages.title: LLM Judges
pages.description: A gallery of ready-to-use LLM-as-a-Judge evaluators for GenGuardX. Browse by category and inspect each judge's prompt and code.

pages.title: GenerativeAI Lifecycle Management
pages.description: Use GGX to manage the GenAI lifecycle from use case definition and data collection through pipeline development, evaluation, approval, deployment, and production monitoring.

pages.title: Collaboration
pages.description: How teams collaborate in GGX — sharing and requesting object access, role-based access management, external sync, groups, workspaces, and monitoring dashboards.

pages.title: Pipeline Registration Guide
pages.description: Register an intent classification pipeline in GGX by connecting a model and prompt, configuring pipeline metadata, adding resources, and testing outputs.

pages.title: Prompt Registration Guide
pages.description: Register an intent classification prompt in GGX by defining prompt metadata, template variables, structured instructions, examples, and test cases.

pages.title: Pipeline Registration Guide: English to French Translation
pages.description: Register an English-to-French translation pipeline in GGX using Gemini 2.0 Flash, custom translation logic, pipeline metadata, and usage tracking.

pages.title: Model Registration: Gemini 2.0 Flash
pages.description: Register Gemini 2.0 Flash in the GGX Model Catalog with provider settings, input arguments, scoring logic, model metadata, and test examples.

pages.title: Inventory Management
pages.description: Register, organize, govern, share, version, and monitor GGX data and GenAI assets including tables, models, prompts, RAGs, and end-to-end pipelines.

pages.title: Global Functions
pages.description: How Global Functions work in GGX — reusable analytical logic with inputs and outputs of any type, registered once and called across pipelines, RAGs, models, reports, and simulations.

pages.title: Model Catalog
pages.description: How models work in GGX — API-based, Python-based, and custom-uploaded models, how to register one, the supported providers, how to test a model, and a worked example.

pages.title: Pipelines
pages.description: How pipelines work in GGX — compose Models, RAGs, Prompts and Guardrails with orchestration logic. Covers pipeline types, anatomy, registration steps, testing, and risk assessment.

pages.title: Prompt Registry
pages.description: How prompts work in GGX — the template, input arguments, and creation logic that make up a registered prompt, how to register one, how to test and improve it, and a worked example.

pages.title: RAGs
pages.description: How RAGs work in GGX — the knowledge source and retrieval logic that make up a registered RAG, how to register one, and what registering it unlocks.

pages.title: Data Assets
pages.description: Register data tables and quality checks in GGX so teams can track source data, fetch schemas, run data quality reports, audit changes, and reuse validation datasets.

pages.title: Lineage Tracking
pages.description: Use GGX lineage tracking to visualize object dependencies, identify upstream and downstream impact, support traceability, and understand how registered assets are used.

pages.title: Prompt Optimization
pages.description: How GGX automates prompt optimization with Hill Climbing — iteratively refining a prompt, keeping only changes that improve a fixed evaluation, with full logs and one-click sync back to the pipeline.

pages.title: GGX Sync
pages.description: Use GGX Sync to declare, version, and synchronize prompts, models, RAGs, pipelines, global functions, and reports from Python code into GenGuardX.

pages.title: Version Management
pages.description: How GGX versions objects — the Draft → Approved → Clone lifecycle, snapshot-based Change History, reverting to any point, and how versions propagate downstream.

pages.title: GGX Monitoring Benchmarks
pages.description: Benchmark GGX monitoring at scale across observability ingestion, heuristic triage, parallel LLM judges, automated routing, and targeted human review.

pages.title: Self-Hosted GGX
pages.description: Install, configure, scale, back up, harden, and operate self-hosted GGX instances across Kubernetes, Terraform, cloud, Docker, and manual deployment options.

pages.title: CI CD Integrations
pages.description: Integrate GGX approval workflows with external CI/CD and review systems by implementing custom approval handlers and exchanging review actions through APIs.

pages.title: Common Configs
pages.description: Configure common self-hosted GGX settings for application services, API behavior, storage, authentication, workers, notebooks, logging, and deployment environments.

pages.title: Metadata: Database Setup
pages.description: Configure GGX metadata database connections for PostgreSQL, Oracle, SQL Server, and related self-hosted database settings, migrations, and operational requirements.

pages.title: Datalake Integration
pages.description: Configure GGX data lake access for self-hosted deployments so jobs, reports, and analytics can read and write approved data locations.

pages.title: Email Notifications
pages.description: Configure email notification settings for self-hosted GGX so approvals, alerts, reviews, and system events can reach the right users.

pages.title: Additional Libraries
pages.description: Install and manage additional Python libraries for self-hosted GGX components, notebooks, workers, and analytical workloads.

pages.title: Process Management
pages.description: Run self-hosted GGX services under process managers such as systemd or Supervisor to manage startup, restarts, logs, and long-running components.

pages.title: SAML
pages.description: Configure SAML single sign-on for self-hosted GGX with enterprise identity providers, authentication settings, and user access requirements.

pages.title: Web Server Setup
pages.description: Configure Nginx, Apache, or other reverse proxies for self-hosted GGX, including routing, TLS, timeouts, compression, and application endpoints.

pages.title: Hardening - Security
pages.description: Harden self-hosted GGX deployments with security controls for network access, secrets, authentication, infrastructure, data storage, and operational practices.

pages.title: AWS
pages.description: Deploy self-hosted GGX on AWS using ECS Fargate, EFS, load balancers, networking, IAM, database services, and container registry configuration.

pages.title: Azure
pages.description: Deploy self-hosted GGX on Azure using Container Apps, Azure Files, managed networking, database services, container registry, and Terraform-based infrastructure.

pages.title: Docker-based
pages.description: Run self-hosted GGX with Docker-based deployments for local, staging, or controlled environments using containers, volumes, configuration, and service orchestration.

pages.title: GCP
pages.description: Deploy self-hosted GGX on Google Cloud using Cloud Run, GKE, Cloud SQL, Cloud Storage, networking, service accounts, and Terraform modules.

pages.title: Kubernetes
pages.description: Deploy self-hosted GGX on Kubernetes using Kustomize manifests, namespaces, persistent volumes, ingress, secrets, and provider-specific cluster settings.

pages.title: Manual
pages.description: Install self-hosted GGX manually on VMs, bare metal, or cloud instances with direct control over services, databases, storage, web servers, and process managers.

pages.title: Minimum Requirements
pages.description: Review the minimum CPU, memory, storage, database, Spark, Python, Java, web server, and process management requirements for self-hosted GGX components.

pages.title: Terraform
pages.description: Provision self-hosted GGX infrastructure as code with Terraform modules for AWS, Azure, and Google Cloud managed container deployments.

pages.title: Backups & Restore
pages.description: Back up and restore self-hosted GGX metadata databases, file management storage, data lake outputs, settings, and component configuration files.

pages.title: Multiple GGX Workers
pages.description: Run multiple named GGX workers with separate queues, process files, logs, state databases, and worker-specific configuration in self-hosted environments.

pages.title: Scalability and Sizing
pages.description: Plan self-hosted GGX scalability for production throughput, simulation jobs, user analytics, application services, databases, APIs, and worker capacity.
